Politics.headline: Trump Announces Naval Blockade of Iran After Islamabad Talks Yield No Deal
Politics.detail: President Trump announced the U.S. is imposing a naval blockade on Iran and the Strait of Hormuz after peace talks in Pakistan ended in failure, aiming to deny Iran leverage and prevent it from exporting oil. Vice President JD Vance said the U.S. remains open to diplomacy if Iran takes 'our final and best offer.'

Politics.headline: Hungary's Viktor Orban Concedes Election Defeat
Politics.detail: Viktor Orban, a long-time ally of Trump and Putin, conceded election defeat after 16 years in power. With record turnout, Hungarians chose to end the rule of the prime minister who was a self-proclaimed champion of illiberal Christian democracy.

Politics.headline: Eric Swalwell Suspends Campaign for California Governor After Sex Assault Allegations
Politics.detail: Eric Swalwell has suspended his campaign for California governor after allegations of sexual assault by an ex-staffer. Several Democratic and Republican lawmakers said they would be in favor of voting to expel Swalwell, as well as Rep. Tony Gonzales (R-Texas), who both face misconduct allegations.

Politics.headline: Liberals Will Keep Working Across Party Lines Even If Byelections Deliver Majority: MacKinnon
Politics.detail: As the Liberals gear up for three federal byelections on Monday that could deliver them a majority government, House leader Steven MacKinnon says the government will 'continue that impulse of working across party lines' even if it's not required to co-operate to pass legislation.

Politics.headline: Calls Grow for Rep. Swalwell to Resign or Be Expelled Over Misconduct Claims
Politics.detail: Several Democratic and Republican lawmakers said they would be in favor of voting to expel Swalwell, as well as Rep. Tony Gonzales (R-Texas), who both face misconduct allegations. An accusation of sexual assault against Rep. Eric Swalwell (D-California) by.

Finance & Economy.headline: U.S. to begin Iran port blockade on April 13, CENTCOM says
Finance & Economy.detail: Regional countries are working to bring the United States and Iran back to the negotiating table after marathon talks in Islamabad ended without an agreement, according to the Wall Street Journal, citing officials familiar with the discussions. The report said the door remains open .

Finance & Economy.headline: Oil prices jump above $100/barrel after Trump orders Hormuz blockade
Finance & Economy.detail: Oil prices surged after President Trump announced that the U.S. would begin a blockade of Iranian ports. The blockade follows unsuccessful peace talks and rising tensions in the region.

Finance & Economy.headline: UK chancellor warns of 'cost to British families, businesses' of war
Finance & Economy.detail: UK Chancellor Rachel Reeves has warned 'the war in Iran will come at a cost to British families and businesses'. The Chancellor made the statement following the failure of US-Iran peace talks.

Finance & Economy.headline: Bank of Canada rate hike bets look 'unrealistic' given 'sclerotic' state of jobs market, say economists
Finance & Economy.detail: Economists suggest that the Bank of Canada is unlikely to raise interest rates due to the slow growth in the jobs market. The Canadian unemployment rate remained unchanged after the economy added only 14,000 jobs.

Finance & Economy.headline: IMCO's CoreWeave bet powers 7.4% annual gain
Finance & Economy.detail: The Investment Management Corporation of Ontario (IMCO) saw a 7.4% annual gain, boosted by its investment in CoreWeave. The pension manager's stock holdings gained 19.1% in 2025, while private equity rose 6.5%.

Technology.headline: Universities of Wisconsin regents cite disputes over AI and other topics in president's firing
Technology.detail: Disagreements over the use and implementation of Artificial Intelligence contributed to the decision to fire the president of the Universities of Wisconsin. The regents cited these disputes, among other topics, as key factors in their decision.

Technology.headline: AI models can fake visual understanding of images that don't exist
Technology.detail: New research indicates that AI models can generate outputs that suggest a visual understanding of images, even when those images are entirely fabricated or nonexistent. This raises concerns about the potential for AI to create convincing but false visual information.

Technology.headline: Sutter Health and MemorialCare Hit With Class Action Over AI Transcription
Technology.detail: Sutter Health and MemorialCare are facing a class-action lawsuit over their alleged use of AI transcription technology to record physician-patient conversations without proper consent. The lawsuit claims that microphone-enabled devices in examination rooms were used to capture and transcribe these conversations.

Technology.headline: Elon Musk's xAI sues Colorado over new rules for artificial intelligence.
Technology.detail: Elon Musk's xAI has filed a lawsuit against Colorado concerning the state's recently introduced regulations for artificial intelligence. The lawsuit challenges the legal framework and restrictions imposed on AI development and deployment within the state.

Technology.headline: One tiny diode could shrink image sensors by adding memory and processing
Technology.detail: Researchers have developed a miniature diode that could potentially revolutionize image sensors by integrating memory and processing capabilities. This innovation could lead to significantly smaller and more efficient image sensors in various devices.

Science.headline: Extinct ice age giants in Bender's Cave challenge existing climate records for the Edwards Plateau
Science.detail: New research from Bender's Cave suggests existing climate records for the Edwards Plateau may need to be revised based on the analysis of extinct ice age giants found within the cave.

Science.headline: Astronomers find the strongest evidence yet for the universe's first stars
Science.detail: Astronomers have discovered the most compelling evidence so far of the universe's earliest stars, providing insights into the formation of the cosmos.

Science.headline: Ancient Korean DNA reveals marriages between closely related individuals
Science.detail: Analysis of ancient Korean DNA has revealed evidence of marriages between closely related individuals in ancient Korean society.

Science.headline: Space telescopes track nearby quasar's dramatic X-ray state transition
Science.detail: Space telescopes are monitoring a nearby quasar's dramatic shift in its X-ray state, providing valuable data on the behavior of these energetic celestial objects.

Science.headline: A new study reveals that popular diabetes and weight-loss drugs like Ozempic and Wegovy may not work as effectively for about 10% of people due to specific genetic variants.
Science.detail: According to a recent study, genetic variations can impact the effectiveness of diabetes and weight-loss medications like Ozempic and Wegovy in approximately 10% of individuals.

Health.headline: Fried nuggets and steamed sponges off menu in school food overhaul in England
Health.detail: Campaigners welcome first update of school food standards in 13 years, which aims to help lower obesity rates.

Health.headline: Votes for populist parties in May elections will put NHS at risk, Streeting says
Health.detail: Health secretary warns of dangers of protest vote as he pitches NHS as key elections battleground.

Health.headline: Food delivery for heart failure patients shows high uptake, may boost quality of life
Health.detail: A study suggests that providing food delivery services to heart failure patients is well-received and may improve their overall quality of life.

Health.headline: Immune cells in the nose slow influenza virus, study finds
Health.detail: Research indicates that immune cells present in the nasal passages can effectively slow down the influenza virus.

Health.headline: As RSV evolves, a two‑pronged antibody cocktail aims to stay ahead
Health.detail: Scientists are developing a two-pronged antibody cocktail to combat the evolving respiratory syncytial virus (RSV).

Sports.headline: Sinner beats Alcaraz in straight sets in Monte Carlo final to reclaim No. 1 ranking
Sports.detail: Jannik Sinner defeated Carlos Alcaraz in straight sets to win the Monte Carlo Masters final and regain the No. 1 ranking in men's tennis.

Sports.headline: Sawan Barwal breaks Shivnath Singh's 48-year-old marathon national record
Sports.detail: Sawan Barwal has broken Shivnath Singh's 48-year-old national record in the marathon.

Sports.headline: IPL 2026 | Samson rises to the challenge as Super Kings get going, at last
Sports.detail: Sanju Samson played a key role in the Super Kings' recent IPL match.

Sports.headline: Barnes's triple-double powers Raptors past Nets, clinching Toronto's first playoff berth in 4 years
Sports.detail: Scottie Barnes's triple-double performance helped the Toronto Raptors beat the Nets and secure their first playoff appearance in 4 years.

Sports.headline: Rory repeats: McIlroy goes back-to-back at the Masters to join Jack Nicklaus, Nick Faldo and Tiger Woods
Sports.detail: Rory McIlroy won the Masters for the second consecutive year, joining legendary golfers Jack Nicklaus, Nick Faldo, and Tiger Woods.

Entertainment.headline: Cast of Celebrity Treasure Island 2026 announced
Entertainment.detail: A new group of Kiwi celebrities are set to compete in intense physical challenges on Celebrity Treasure Island 2026, vying for a $100,000 charity prize. The reality series, hosted by Bree Tomasel and Jayden Daniels, will take place against the backdrop of Northland, New Zealand.

Entertainment.headline: Director Na Hong-jin's upcoming film 'Hope' to be distributed by U.S. indie powerhouse NEON
Entertainment.detail: Na Hong-jin's upcoming film “Hope” will be distributed in North America by NEON, known for distributing international art-house hits. The film, set in a remote outpost near the demilitarized zone, involves a local official and reports of a tiger, spiraling into an encounter with an “unbelievable reality”.

Entertainment.headline: Box Office Report [April 12, 2026]: Dacoit, Love Insurance Kompany and Dhurandhar 2's Sunday earnings
Entertainment.detail: The Indian box office is seeing competition between new South Indian films like Dacoit and LIK: Love Insurance Kompany, while Ranveer Singh's Dhurandhar: The Revenge continues to dominate. Dhurandhar 2 earned Rs 14.75 crore on Sunday, bringing its total collection to Rs 1,083.67 crore.

Entertainment.headline: Entertainment from Monday, April 13th, 2026 - Dubai Eye 103.8 - News, Talk & Sports
Entertainment.detail: Dubai Eye 103.8 featured segments on the UAE's housing supply, highlighting Dubai's population growth. They also interviewed Noon's first female delivery driver, Glory Ehirim Nkiruka, about her job.

Entertainment.headline: Ranbir Kapoor Leads Ambitious Epic 'Ramayana' I AP Interview
Entertainment.detail: Ranbir Kapoor says that while he doesn't love the promotion side of his job, he's happy to do it for “Ramayana.” The Indian star leads an ambitious new two-part film epic aiming to share the sacred Hindu stories with audiences around the world.

Crime & Justice.headline: Four children killed in nursery school stabbing attack in Uganda
Crime & Justice.detail: Police say a suspect is in custody but the motive remains unknown after attack in Ugandan capital, Kampala.

Crime & Justice.headline: Machete-wielding man killed by police in New York's Grand Central station
Crime & Justice.detail: Three elderly victims were wounded at the transit hub attack as authorities continue to investigate, police said.

Crime & Justice.headline: Malnourished boy found locked in utility van since 2024, police say
Crime & Justice.detail: Police discovered a severely malnourished boy who had been confined to a utility van since 2024, prompting an investigation into the disturbing case of child neglect.

Crime & Justice.headline: Richmond Hill fatal stabbing under investigation by NYPD
Crime & Justice.detail: A 41-year-old man was stabbed to death and a 32-year-old woman was wounded in Queens; police are investigating and looking for a male suspect in a gray hoodie who fled the scene.

Crime & Justice.headline: Police arrest 21 suspects in hospice fraud case
Crime & Justice.detail: Authorities have arrested 21 individuals suspected of involvement in a widespread hospice fraud scheme.

Education.headline: 'I feel helpless': college graduates can't find entry-level roles in shrinking market amid rise of AI.
Education.detail: Many college graduates are struggling to find entry-level positions due to a shrinking market and the increasing presence of artificial intelligence in the workforce.

Education.headline: NASUWT: Pay and pupil violence on agenda as teachers gather in Birmingham
Education.detail: The National Association of Schoolmasters Union of Women Teachers (NASUWT) is meeting in Birmingham to discuss issues such as teacher pay and pupil violence.

Education.headline: Government launches new research funding to strengthen school food programs across Canada
Education.detail: The Canadian government has announced new research funding aimed at improving school food programs throughout the country.

Education.headline: School & District Management Opinion Formative Assessments Are Top of Mind for Teachers. They Should Be for Principals, Too.
Education.detail: A recent article emphasizes the importance of formative assessments for teachers and argues that principals should also prioritize them.

Education.headline: Education secretary used fake photo in post about Black history icon.
Education.detail: Linda McMahon's Truth Social post about journalist Ida B. Wells is the latest problematic use of AI and other digitally created images by the Trump administration.

Lifestyle.headline: Inside NZ's ute culture: tradition, community and a changing future
Lifestyle.detail: The humble ute has evolved into a lifestyle staple shaping how Kiwis work, travel and live, with women playing an increasing role in ute culture, but rising fuel costs pose a challenge to leisure use.

Lifestyle.headline: Where Tokyo goes to sweat and soak
Lifestyle.detail: In Tokyo, public bathhouses are evolving into social spaces, attracting younger generations with music, design, and nightlife elements, offering a temporary escape from the digital world.

Lifestyle.headline: The Loop PH enters a new era with refreshed identity and lifestyle-tech vision
Lifestyle.detail: The Loop is rebranding from a device retailer to a lifestyle-tech partner, catering to modern consumers seeking technology that integrates into their daily lives.

Lifestyle.headline: Top 3 to get full Limkokwing Scholarship
Lifestyle.detail: The top 3 winners of the MTN Bushfire Firefly Youth Talent Competition will receive a full tuition scholarship to Limkokwing University of Creative Technology Eswatini Campus, with the option to study at sister campuses in Lesotho or Botswana.

Lifestyle.headline: New exhibition opens to mark 200 years of Harrogate's first hospital
Lifestyle.detail: A new exhibition has opened in Harrogate to celebrate the 200th anniversary of the town's first hospital, showcasing its history and impact on the community.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: US, Iran fail to reach deal
Opinion/Editorial.detail: Iran and the United States failed to reach an agreement to end the war in the Middle East after marathon talks in Islamabad, according to US Vice President JD Vance. Vance signaled that he was still giving Iran time to consider the offer from the United States, which on Tuesday said it would pause attacks with Israel for two weeks pending negotiations.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: The Guardian view on US-Iran talks: Trump's diplomacy falters as risk of war grows
Opinion/Editorial.detail: Talks between the US and Iran have faltered, increasing the risk of war. The US is seeking a commitment from Iran not to develop nuclear weapons.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Be cautious of China 'incentive': expert
Opinion/Editorial.detail: A trade academic suggests China's new incentives for Taiwan might blur the lines between politics and business, warning of potential trade bans and the policy's aim to sow division within Taiwan by portraying the government as hindering development. The measures include facilitating sales of Taiwanese agricultural and fishery products and promoting travel to Taiwan.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: When a mayor becomes a symbol in France's far-right politics
Opinion/Editorial.detail: The story reflects what some describe as a breakdown in mainstream media standards and the rise of inflammatory discourse driven by hate speech and Islamophobia. A sense of ostracism is increasingly directed at politicians of Arab and Sub-Saharan African descent, with attempts to demonize voices critical of the political and media establishment, particularly within far-right media and political circles.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: U.S. announces blockade of Hormuz from today; Asha Bhosle's last rites to be held at Shivaji Park today, and more
Opinion/Editorial.detail: The U.S. military will start a blockade of all ships going to and from Iran. The last rites of legendary singer Asha Bhosle will be performed at Shivaji Park in Mumbai with state honors.

World.headline: Trump says US will blockade strait of Hormuz after Iran peace talks fail
World.detail: Following the collapse of peace talks between the US and Iran, President Trump announced that the US will blockade the Strait of Hormuz. The US Navy will enforce the blockade after Trump condemned Iran for blocking the waterway.

World.headline: Hungarian opposition ousts Viktor Orbán after 16 years in power
World.detail: After 16 years in power, Viktor Orbán was unseated as Hungary's leader. Péter Magyar and his party Tisza won the election in a resounding victory.

World.headline: US military threatens to blockade all Iranian ports starting on Monday
World.detail: The US military has announced it will blockade all Iranian ports starting on Monday. This action follows the failure of US-Iran ceasefire talks.

World.headline: At least 30 killed in crush at historic fortress in Haiti
World.detail: A stampede at Haiti's historic Citadelle Laferriere has resulted in the deaths of at least 30 people. The incident adds to the troubles in a country struggling with gang violence and political instability.

World.headline: Oil prices surge past $103 a barrel after US announces blockade of Iran
World.detail: Following the US announcement of a blockade of Iran, oil prices have surged past $103 a barrel. The blockade has injected fresh turmoil into financial markets, causing Asian stocks to fall.

Zimbabwe.headline: Mnangagwa sacks rights commission chair after constitutional amendment rebuke
Zimbabwe.detail: Lawyers say president's move to “reassign” her is unconstitutional and void after she criticised the controversial Constitution Amendment Bill No. 3.

Zimbabwe.headline: Robbers disarm police, steal rifle and patrol vehicle in Harare attack
Zimbabwe.detail: Officers were hospitalised for injuries sustained during the brazen midnight attack in Harare.

Zimbabwe.headline: Jessie Majome Was Reassigned Before: Presidential Spokesperson Hits Back At Lawyers Over ZHRC Move
Zimbabwe.detail: A presidential spokesperson has responded to lawyers concerning the reassignment of Jessie Majome from the Zimbabwe Human Rights Commission (ZHRC).

Zimbabwe.headline: From US$370 To US$897: Here Are Zimbabwe's Reviewed Salaries For Civil Servants Starting 1 April
Zimbabwe.detail: Civil servants in Zimbabwe have received reviewed salaries starting April 1, with amounts ranging from US$370 to US$897.

Zimbabwe.headline: Dismissals And Travel Bans: New Law Punishes Councils For Failing Basic Services – Minister Garwe
Zimbabwe.detail: A new law in Zimbabwe introduces dismissals and travel bans as punishment for councils failing to provide basic services, according to Minister Garwe.
